summary: PR #2559 made focused matching changes in one fighter file and one stage file. The bot report says two functions newly matched: ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA and grGreens_80214B58. The PR body notes CaptureWaitKirby could be linked if data were figured out but shares strings with thrownkirby; reviewer feedback states shared strings mean the units have to be merged.

key_files.role: Fighter-side CaptureWaitKirby matching work; diff removes unused includes/static constants, adds a noinline wrapper around ftCo_800BD6EC, adjusts ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA call ordering/arguments, and changes scale source to fp->mv.co.capturekirby.scale.
Stage-side grGreens_80214B58 matching work; diff rewrites BOMB as a nested ternary assignment and changes a 0x20 clearing loop into a 24-iteration loop plus explicit stores for 0x18 through 0x1D.

what_changed: Removed placeholder/platform and several unused fighter/baselib/dolphin includes from ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c.
Removed local static float/double constants ftCo_804D8BB8, ftCo_804D8BBC, ftCo_804D8BC0, and ftCo_804D8BC8 from ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c.
Added ftCo_800BD6EC_noinline as a wrapper that calls ftCo_800BD6EC, then used that wrapper in ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA before inlineB0.
Changed inlineB0 to compute an s32 direction value of -1 or 1 from lstick.x and pass that to ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04.
Changed ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A38 call in ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA from passing fp to passing gobj->user_data.
Added an s32 pad local in ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA.
Rewrote grGreens BOMB helper to assign grGr_8049F9E0[idx] via a single nested ternary expression using grGr_params->x20 and HSD_Randi.
Changed grGreens_80214B58 zero initialization from a loop over 0x20 entries to a loop over 24 entries plus explicit zero stores for indices 0x18 through 0x1D.
Bot report recorded +1644 matched-code bytes and two newly matched items.

smart_moves.text: Kept the PR focused to two functions reported by the bot as newly matching, one in ftCommon and one in grgreens.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists 2 new matches

smart_moves.text: Removed generated-looking include and local constant clutter from ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c while preserving the matching target.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: include removals and ftCo_804D8BB8/ftCo_804D8BC0 removals

smart_moves.text: Flagged remaining data/string ownership uncertainty in the PR body instead of presenting CaptureWaitKirby as fully linkable.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body says CaptureWaitKirby could be linked if data was figured out but shares strings with thrownkirby

decomp_lessons.lesson: String/data ownership can block linking even when a function matches; shared strings between related fighter units may imply a TU merge or split-ownership issue rather than a simple code-only fix.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author notes shared strings with thrownkirby
human_pr_text: ribbanya says shared strings have to be merged

decomp_lessons.lesson: A local noinline wrapper can be a narrow matching tactic when direct calls disturb code shape, but this PR only proves that the wrapper was present in a bot-reported matching result, not that the tactic is generally preferred.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added ftCo_800BD6EC_noinline wrapper and called it from ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA

decomp_lessons.lesson: For array clearing code, a matching shape may split a loop from trailing explicit stores; keep this as PR-specific evidence and do not generalize it over the natural-loop standard without verifier evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGreens_80214B58 loop changed from i < 0x20 to i < 24 plus explicit stores for 0x18-0x1D

naming_conventions.convention: No new broad naming convention is established. The PR keeps address-style function names such as ftCo_800BD6EC and grGreens_80214B58, and uses a mechanical suffix ftCo_800BD6EC_noinline for a wrapper.
Local role names remain minimal and codegen-oriented where semantics are limited, such as value, pad, i, jobj, and fp.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: s32 value, s32 pad, Fighter* fp, HSD_JObj* jobj

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a noinline wrapper around an existing helper to alter call/inlining behavior in ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA.
Add a pad local in ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA, likely for stack/codegen shaping; PR evidence does not explain it beyond the diff and bot match.
Rewrite a helper assignment into a single nested ternary expression in BOMB to shape grgreens codegen.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: BOMB changed from c/r temporaries to nested ternary assignment

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace full array-clear loop with a shorter loop plus explicit stores for trailing indices in grGreens_80214B58.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: loop i < 24 plus grGr_8049F9E0[0x18] through [0x1D] stores

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use struct field access for the scale argument, changing HSD_JObjSetScale from &jobj->scale to &fp->mv.co.capturekirby.scale.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: If they share strings then they have to be merged.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Approved review recorded with empty body.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: review by ribbanya state APPROVED

raw_diff: full diff excerpt for both changed files
text_corpus: PR body, bot report, issue comment, approved review
notes: Evidence is strong for changed files, exact code edits, bot-reported match results, and reviewer text.
No inline review comments were present.
The PR body states data/string uncertainty but does not include the underlying string/data map evidence.
The bot report supports match percentages, but this intake did not independently run builds, objdiff, or regression checks.
The reason for pad/noinline tactics is inferred from matching context and should not be treated as author-stated intent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR #2559 matched ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA and grGreens_80214B58
accepted_candidate_records.text: The bot report for GALE01 says PR #2559 produced two new matches: ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby and grGreens_80214B58 in main/melee/gr/grgreens.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Shared strings raised as merge/split concern
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR author noted CaptureWaitKirby shares strings with thrownkirby, and reviewer ribbanya responded that if they share strings then they have to be merged.

source_update_candidates.title: Shared string clusters may indicate TU merge or split ownership work
source_update_candidates.text: When two candidate translation units share strings, investigate section ownership and possible TU merging/splitting instead of forcing a standalone code link. This should be framed as data/TU ownership evidence, not as a blanket rule that every shared string requires a merge without corroborating map/split evidence.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says CaptureWaitKirby could be linked if data was figured out but shares strings with thrownkirby
human_pr_text: ribbanya says shared strings have to be merged
source_update_candidates.reason: Reviewer feedback and author note provide a concrete historical example of shared strings affecting whether a code-matching unit can be linked independently.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the noinline wrapper or s32 pad as a general accepted style; the PR evidence only shows they were used in this matching diff.
Do not promote the grgreens loop-plus-explicit-stores shape as a replacement for the natural-loop standard; this is a localized matching shape with bot evidence only.
Do not claim CaptureWaitKirby was fully linked; the PR body explicitly says data remained an issue.
Do not infer detailed thrownkirby file paths or string symbols from this slice; only the name thrownkirby and shared-string concern are present.
